programmes.show_description: Ford 100E/Ford Escort MK1: Derek is reunited with the vehicle he took his driving test in - a 1957 Ford 100E Estate. Dave is also on the trail for an Escort, this time a 1970s Mk1 twin cam.
Morris T2/Alfa Romeo Sud: Derek's search for the unusual lands him a 1947 Lea Francis that's half-made of wood. Paul heads for the countryside to pick up a Morris one tonne truck.

Austin Healey Lenham Six/VW Beetle: Dave and Jack head to East Yorkshire to pick up a dusty Alfa Romeo and a 1930s Jowett. But Dave's more taken with a Carry On-style, bum-wobbling exercise machine. S
Shopping from Home.
Biggin Hill to Ashdown Forest: Michael Portillo takes a trip in a Second World War Spitfire above Biggin Hill aerodrome.
Hassocks To Benenden: Michael Portillo arrives in Hassocks and the beautiful Sussex village of Ditchling, before making his way to the village of Benenden.
Rye to Dungeness: Michael Portillo follows his 1930s Bradshaw's guide to the unspoilt East Sussex port of Rye, then boards the Romney, Hythe and Dymchurch railway to Dungeness.
Deal to Margate: Michael Portillo heads to Walmer Castle, home during the 1920s to Lord Beauchamp, and in Margate he discovers the connection to TS Eliot.
A New Germany: Legendary actor Laurence Olivier narrates the definitive series on World War II. Hear why Germany, stricken by defeat in WWI, looked to Adolf Hitler for hope.
Distant War: Improve your knowledge of WWII with this acclaimed series narrated by Laurence Olivier. As the Nazi war machine sweeps across Europe, an uneasy calm settles on Britain.
France Falls: Laurence Olivier narrates this epic, 26-part WWII retrospective. Hear how French military leaders made the disastrous mistake of preparing for a war similar to WWI.
Barton Swing Aqueduct to Liverpool: Robbie Cumming concludes his journey by travelling from Barton Swing Aqueduct to Liverpool Docks - but experiences engine problems on the way.
Ellesmere Port to Audlem: Robbie Cumming battles his way through blanket weed on the Shropshire Union Canal and discovers industrial secrets in Audlem in Cheshire.
Market Drayton to Stourport-on-Severn: Robbie gets stuck in the mud in Woodseaves Cutting and explores the charming canal-side village of Kinver in Staffordshire.
Stourport Basins to Kingswood Junction: Robbie navigates the mighty River Severn and takes an unexpected bath as he takes a tumble at the Tardebigge lock flight in Worcestershire.
New. Martin heads to New York, exploring Manhattan and Ellis Island. His final stop is Maine's remote islands, where he joins a century-old service providing support to isolated islanders.
New. Two of the country's best-loved comedians, Joanna Lumley and Jennifer Saunders, take a trip to France to find out how to put the bubbles into bubbly.
Simon Reeve's journey around Turkey begins at Istanbul and the beaches of the Aegean Sea before ending up at the war-torn border with Syria.
Simon Reeve travels along the Black Sea Coast to the capital of Ankara, and witnesses first hand the aftermath of fighting in Kurdish areas.
Knottingley to Ripon: On the last leg of his epic journey across the north of England, Robbie tackles the fast-flowing River Ouse and is forced to make an emergency stop.
Sheffield to Keadby: Robbie Cumming makes a running repair to his narrowboat on the Tinsley lock flight in South Yorkshire, then cruises beneath Conisbrough's magnificent viaduct.
Keadby to Kiveton Park: Robbie Cumming tackles the fast-flowing River Trent and is invited to join a night-time illuminated boat parade.
Lincoln to Nottingham: Narrowboater Robbie Cumming faces a journey of many challenges as he runs aground and breaks down in Nottingham.
The workshop gets a new wall and floor. Meanwhile, Sam Lovegrove mends Dom's vintage motorbike for a nail-biting cliff top trial. Plus, cider, an orchard and an auction are on the menu.
...Lads? Stranger on a Train: Chucklesome sequel to The Likely Lads. After five years apart, the old pals meet by chance. Terry returns to civilian life, while marriage looms for Bob.
...Lads? Home Is the Hero: Brilliant 'buddy sitcom' from the early 1970s. Terry's return has been less than triumphant - his parents are away, it's cold and there's no food in the house.
...Lads? Cold Feet: Inspired 70s sitcom from the creators of Porridge. Bob is embroiled in preparations for his wedding to Thelma, but Terry's return has caused him to have doubts.
Willys Jeep/Austin A35/Case Tractor/Hummer: Derek picks up some very special working vehicles. Charlie feels the pull of a gargantuan Hummer and Paul extracts a vintage tractor from a Yorkshire barn.
Rolls Royce/Impreza/Jag/Fords: There's Hollywood glamour in the air as Derek heads out to pick up a Rolls Royce that once belonged to Fred Astaire.
Healey/Porsche Soapbox: Austin Healeys are classic British vehicles that are difficult to tame. Paul and Charlie have the rare chance to pick up a trio of these iconic cars in varying conditions. S10
Buses/Ladyline/Norton Jubilee: Derek's got his work cut out collecting a huge haul that includes two Bentleys, a Daimler and a traditional Bowtop caravan.
Merc/Unicycle/Sinclair C5/Golf/AWO: Dave has to extricate a VW Golf from a garden that has grown up around it.
Shopping from Home.
Herne Bay to Leeds Castle: Michael Portillo reaches the Kent seaside resort of Herne Bay, eventually crosses the North Downs to Lenham, and pays a visit to Leeds Castle.
Chislehurst to Kennington: Michael Portillo continues his travels in Greater London, ending up in Kennington to learn about the childhood of Charlie Chaplin.
Hackney Wick to Oxford Circus: Michael Portillo continues his travels through the capital in the heart of London's East End, making his way from Hackney Wick to Oxford Circus.
Hampstead to Islington: Michael Portillo travels to Hampstead, where he learns more about the founder of psychoanalysis, Sigmund Freud, before making his way to Islington.
AA Bikes/MGA/Terrapin: Derek picks up a quartet of tasty vehicles from a farm in Shropshire. Among them a 1937 Austin Seven van and a Triumph TR2 from the mid-50s.
Albion/Lambrettas/Cosworth: Paul and his son Charlie head to Leicestershire to collect a vintage Albion truck. Steeped in nostalgia, both Paul and Charlie fall for the Albion's charms.
Alone: After Dunkirk, Britain faced an onslaught. Although the RAF won the Battle of Britain, our cities were blitzed. Laurence Olivier tells us more in the landmark series on WWII.
At the Battle of Mons, the Germans retreat in panic when confronted by a group of angels. Is this divine intervention, mass hallucination - or something even stranger?
Clissold Park 1: Fiona Bruce presents the show from Clissold Park in the London Borough of Hackney, where treasures include some pricey whisky bottles.
Brodie Castle and Estate, Morayshire 2: Fiona Bruce presents the show from the grounds of Brodie Castle in Scotland, where treasures include football memorabilia.
Rolls Royce / Honda C70: The team fly the flag for King and country with one of their most luxurious purchases to date - a 1981 Rolls Royce Silver Spirit.
VW Beetle / Royal Enfield Cycar: The team think they can smell profit by choosing one of the simplest cars ever built, the VW Beetle.
Alone: After Dunkirk, Britain faced an onslaught. Although the RAF won the Battle of Britain, our cities were blitzed. Laurence Olivier tells us more in the landmark series on WWII.
At the Battle of Mons, the Germans retreat in panic when confronted by a group of angels. Is this divine intervention, mass hallucination - or something even stranger?
Clissold Park 1: Fiona Bruce presents the show from Clissold Park in the London Borough of Hackney, where treasures include some pricey whisky bottles.
Setting off from a snowy mountain ski resort in Cyprus, Simon Reeve finds an island and a capital city still deeply divided between Turkish Cypriots and Greek Cypriots.
Dom finishes the summer with new refurbished windows sealing the weather out of the workshop. Later, he and Sam head to St Agnes to buy Cornish tin to cast a workshop sign.
Simon looks back on wildlife stories he has discovered, from a giant tortoise sanctuary in the Seychelles to armed conservationists defending a forest in Belize.
Rolls Royce / Honda C70: The team fly the flag for King and country with one of their most luxurious purchases to date - a 1981 Rolls Royce Silver Spirit.
VW Beetle / Royal Enfield Cycar: The team think they can smell profit by choosing one of the simplest cars ever built, the VW Beetle.
